chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram

text: 2609 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْكَاهِلِيِّ قَالَ : سَأَلَهُ رَجُلٌ وَ أَنَا حَاضِرٌ عَنِ اَلثَّوْبِ يَكُونُ مَصْبُوغاً بِالْعُصْفُرِ ثُمَّ يُغْسَلُ أَلْبَسُهُ وَ أَنَا مُحْرِمٌ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ لَيْسَ اَلْعُصْفُرُ مِنَ اَلطِّيبِ وَ لَكِنِّي أَكْرَهُ أَنْ تَلْبَسَ مَا يَشْهَرُكَ بِهِ اَلنَّاسُ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2609 - It was narrated from al-Kahili, who said:
A man asked Imam (as) in my presence about a garment dyed with safflower (‘usfur) that has been washed. "Can I wear it while in the state of Ihram?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, safflower is not considered a fragrance. However, I dislike for you to wear something that makes you stand out among people."
